<!-- GENERATED by squonk-conformance `support_tiers`. Do not edit by hand: change `BuiltinDialect::support_tier` / `ProductSurface::support_tier` and regenerate with `UPDATE_SUPPORT_TIERS=1 cargo nextest run -p squonk-conformance support_tier`. -->

# Supported surface and dialect tiers

Every shipped dialect preset and optional product surface carries a release-contract **support tier**. The tier is a coarser, separate axis from the per-feature `Maturity` (which asks whether a single `FeatureSet` knob's API is stable): it states how strong the *parity evidence* for the whole preset is, and therefore what the stable release promises for it.

## Tiers

- **stable** — production-ready; backed by an *authoritative* source of truth (a real engine/reference-parser differential, the SQL standard itself, or a frozen drift-gated contract artifact) and held to it by an enforced gate.
- **preview** — usable and API-stable, but not a full engine-parity guarantee: a differential/comparison oracle wired at partial coverage or outside the default gate, or a preset constructed to match no single engine by design.
- **experimental** — documentation-derived only, with no differential oracle wired (typically because engine acquisition is blocked). Behaviour may diverge and change without notice.

## Dialect presets

| Preset | Tier | Source of truth |
| --- | --- | --- |
| ansi | stable | standard_reference — ISO/IEC 9075:2016 baseline, held by the structural round-trip property (parse(render(x)) == x), the sqllogictest accept corpus, and a documented PostgreSQL nearest-engine delta ledger (oracle-parity-ansi) |
| postgres | stable | engine_differential — libpg_query pg_query 6.1.1 (PostgreSQL 17); raw-parse-tree differential over the vendored corpus (ParseOnly) |
| mysql | stable | engine_differential — mysql 8.4.10; live-server prepare + parse differential (oracle-mysql) |
| sqlite | stable | engine_differential — sqlite rusqlite 0.40 (bundled SQLite); in-process prepare differential (oracle-engines) |
| duckdb | stable | engine_differential — libduckdb 1.5.4; in-process extract_statements differential (oracle-engines) |
| bigquery | preview | comparison — sqlglot; ParseOnly cross-check over a modelled BigQuery surface; not authoritative (the ZetaSQL reference oracle is blocked — oracle-parity-bigquery) |
| hive | experimental | documentation_derived — dialect-reference library; engine-oracle acquisition blocked (oracle-parity-hive) |
| clickhouse | preview | engine_differential — clickhouse-local 25.5.1; external-process EXPLAIN AST over a partial modelled surface; not yet in the default nightly gate (oracle-clickhouse, external-blocked) |
| databricks | experimental | documentation_derived — dialect-reference library; engine-oracle acquisition blocked (oracle-parity-databricks) |
| mssql | experimental | documentation_derived — dialect-reference library; engine-oracle acquisition blocked (oracle-parity-mssql) |
| snowflake | experimental | documentation_derived — dialect-reference library; engine-oracle acquisition blocked (oracle-parity-snowflake) |
| redshift | experimental | documentation_derived — dialect-reference library; engine-oracle acquisition blocked (oracle-parity-redshift) |
| lenient | preview | constructed — permissive parse-anything union of every dialect surface; matches no single engine by design (oracle-parity-lenient) |

## Product surfaces

| Surface | Tier | Source of truth |
| --- | --- | --- |
| document-render | preview | constructed — documented v1 preview: parse-back + spelling fidelity guaranteed and pinned by the format::coverage fixtures; full-fidelity layout remains future work (see the format module docs) |
| serde-ast-schema | stable | contract_gate — release/schema/wire-schema.v1.json; wire schema v1, drift-gated by the wire_schema test and a frozen compat baseline (docs/schema-contract.md) |
| wasm-bindings | preview | constructed — published npm v1 packages wrap the stable parser and wire schema v1; binding-specific compatibility remains preview |
| python-bindings | preview | constructed — published PyPI v1 wheels wrap the stable parser and wire schema v1; binding-specific compatibility remains preview |
